title: Term Frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter in every <terms_dir>/<id>.md file. Terms are single-definition vocabulary nodes — not full concepts, no progress tracking.

examples.name: Batch Normalization
examples.definition: Technique that normalizes each layer's inputs across a mini-batch to stabilize training and reduce sensitivity to initialization.

x-file-example:
---
id: batch_normalization
collection: term
name: "Batch Normalization"
definition: "Technique that normalizes each layer's inputs across a mini-batch to stabilize training and reduce sensitivity to initialization."
related_concepts:
  - "[[neural_networks]]"
  - "[[optimization]]"
related_terms:
  - "[[layer_normalization]]"
sources:
  - "[[lecun_deep_learning_2015]]"
tags:
  - normalization
  - deep-learning
---

id.description: Snake_case ID matching the filename and used in [[wikilinks]]

collection.description: Node type — used by Obsidian-style apps to filter nodes by collection

name.description: Human-readable term name, capitalised as it appears in domain literature

definition.description: One or two sentence plain-English definition — precise, no jargon in the definition itself

related_concepts.description: [[wikilink]] references to concept nodes this term belongs to or is used by

related_terms.description: [[wikilink]] references to sibling or contrasting term nodes

sources.description: [[wikilink]] references to source nodes where this term was defined or introduced
